events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves flanged joints on a commercial piping project, observed at rough-complete stage. The field condition is "no torque marks/witness lines." ASME PCC-1 provides guidelines for bolted flange assembly, including torque verification. B16.5 covers pipe flanges and flanged fittings. Lack of torque marks/witness lines means there's no evidence that bolts were properly torqued per procedure. This is a non-compliance/defective installation condition.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, can't verify proper bolt tensioning)
Risk: medium to high — improper torque can lead to leaks or joint failure
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 85%
Confidence: high, 0.9
